text: Literally, in the Latin version ‘rhinoceros’ here, and elsewhere ‘unicorn’. The word occurs frequently in Scripture, but the exact meaning of it remains in dispute.
vv. 13, 14. The Latin here does not tally with the Hebrew text, the sense of which is by no means certain.
Literally, ‘clothe his neck with neighings (or perhaps, with thunders)’.
vv. 31-35. In the Hebrew text, these are marked as the first five verses of chapter 40.
